1t turns out that the model
mine at Mariana, Pa, in which
the lives of upward of $50 workmen
were blotted out, was just about as
model as the model mines in
West Virginia and Alabama that re-
Gently Mortified civilization by the
immensely of their death tolls. The
coroner at Mariana has learned
that several small explosions oc-
CURED in the mine recently, but the
facts were hushed up, and that the
men regarded the colliery as dam
gerous place to work, but being UNI
organized they were disinclined to
complain for fear of losing their
jobs.


Just week before the explosion
occurred the men formed a union
and sent for a charter to the head
quarters of the United Mine Work
ers of America at Indianapolis, in
tending to demand that the COM
pany introduce safety appliances
and make other improvements.
That is the information that | re-
ceive from one of the national Off
GERS. Furthermore the records show
that nearly every explosion in
which men were killed wholesale
during the past few years occurred
in nonunion mines.


Do you know that the number of
miners killed in 1907 was 4.S6 for
every 1000 employed? This fact is
revealed in the 1907 report of the
United States geological survey. In
bulletin No. IS, United States bu-
reau of labor, just issued, it is stat
ed that the number of deaths from
accident in the mines of Great Brit
ain are only 1929 per 1000. So the
fatalities among the mine workers
in this country are nearly four
times larger than in Great Britain.


The geological survey report
proudly calls attention to an I'M
precedented increase in the number

vance of the market value of coal

prosperity as to regularity of EN-.

wages, etc, but against all this
there loom like spectres the forms
of SAL killed and wounded sol
diers in the mining division of the
industrial army.


John Mitchell has divided the
coal producing states into three
groups, classified as thoroughly
unionized, partially unionized, ant
practically unorganized. Thus II
the organized states, including
Ohio, Indiana. Illinois and Iowa
